headline: Advancing Quantum Architectures and Foundations
summary: Today's research spans the quantum stack, from the physical realities of underground qubit shielding and fast logical operations to the theoretical foundations of parameterized circuits and holographic secret sharing. Together, these papers push the boundaries of quantum error correction, hardware scalability, and mathematical modeling.

themes.title: Fault Tolerance and Hardware Optimization
themes.body: New techniques address the physical and logical bottlenecks of quantum computing. Researchers introduce fast logical operations in quantum LDPC codes using simple cat states, while another study maps the quantum-classical crossover point for fault-tolerant simulations.

themes.title: Physical Control and Environmental Shielding
themes.body: Bridging the gap between theory and physical implementation, scientists are reverse-engineering single-qubit gates using modulated control fields and characterizing material radiopurity deep underground at SNOLAB to protect superconducting qubits from radiation.

themes.title: Mathematical and Holographic Foundations
themes.body: Theoretical frameworks receive a boost through enriched category theory to model parameterized quantum circuits, alongside a new combinatorial model of holographic quantum secret sharing that analyzes phase transitions in AdS3/CFT2.

connections.body: Both papers address the practical thresholds of quantum scalability, with one optimizing logical operations via LDPC codes and the other establishing the exact physical qubit requirements to outperform classical supercomputers.
